title: Measurement, SI Units, Dimensional Analysis, and Uncertainty · Quantity Clinic: Unit Validation
summary: Measure a room, select materials, convert specifications, and report tolerances for a community repair. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg unit validation increment in a unit-safe materials estimate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
Distinguish resolution, accuracy, precision, tolerance, and measurement uncertainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Unit Validation evidence
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 8 m by 6 m. Calculate its area. Use the result to decide Unit Validation for a unit-safe materials estimate.
steps.why: The Unit Validation result for a unit-safe materials estimate is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 8 m × 6 m. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dimensional consistency to detect formula and data-entry errors before calculation.
